---
title: "World Development Indicators (2022)"
author: "Ayushi Chowksey"
date: today

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: moon
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
  pdf: default

execute:
  echo: false
---


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt


df = pd.read_csv("wdi.csv")
```


# World Development Indicators (2022)


## GDP, Life Expectancy, and Global Development Patterns


Ayushi Chowksey

# Introduction


- This presentation analyses selected indicators from the World Development Indicators (2022).
- Focus: Relationship between GDP per capita and life expectancy.
- Objective: Explore economic and social development patterns across countries.

# GDP vs Life Expectancy


```{python}
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("GDP vs Life Expectancy (2022)")
plt.show()
```


- Strong positive relationship between income and life expectancy.
- Rapid gains at lower income levels.
- Diminishing returns at high income levels.

# Top 10 Countries by GDP per Capita


```{python}
top10 = df.sort_values("gdp_per_capita", ascending=False).head(10)


plt.figure()
plt.bar(top10["country"], top10["gdp_per_capita"])
plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.title("Top 10 Countries by GDP per Capita (2022)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


- High-income economies dominate the upper range.
- Significant inequality across global income levels.
- Highlights concentration of economic wealth.

# Key Takeaways


- Income strongly correlates with development outcomes.
- Improvements in health taper at very high income levels.
- Development is multidimensional — not purely economic.

# Conclusion

- GDP per capita remains a powerful predictor of life expectancy.
- However, structural inequality persists globally.
- Sustainable development requires broader investment beyond income growth.
